action.hypothesis: `lowrank_grouped_residual` at the refreshed low-K target (`K=32`, `EF=12`) improved over the recent weak non-TopK probes but remained far from competitive, so the next informative move is a same-target comparison against the strongest simpler non-gated baseline family. Run `lowrank_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` with the shared `signum`/`LR=1.6e-3`/`AUXK_ALPHA=0` recipe to test whether the grouped routing machinery is hurting low-K quality relative to a cleaner low-rank residual path in the preferred mainline band.
action.summary: Keep the refreshed comparison target fixed at `K=32`, `EF=12` and switch only architecture to `lowrank_residual`. This continues the stagnation-breaking architecture search while prioritizing lower K and a fair same-EF comparison.
action.self_review: This preserves the lower-K mainline target and changes only architecture after the grouped residual refresh remained clearly behind the frontier. The main risk is limited novelty because `lowrank_residual` already has positive evidence at other K/EF points, but that is also why it is the strongest clean comparator for isolating whether extra routing structure is helping or hurting at `K=32`, `EF=12`.
notes_to_memory: Round 74 keeps the refreshed low-K mainline target fixed at `K=32`, `EF=12` and switches only architecture from `lowrank_grouped_residual` to `lowrank_residual`.
This same-EF comparison directly tests whether grouped/routed structure is the source of recent low-K weakness relative to a simpler low-rank residual family with real prior signal.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` is competitive, keep the family fixed and map `K=40` before touching EF.
If `lowrank_residual` at `K=32`, `EF=12` is still weak, pivot to another fundamentally different non-TopK direction in the same mainline band rather than reopening archived TopK-family comparisons.
